questions.question: A recipe for $3 \cdot 4$ pancakes requires $2 \cdot 3$ eggs. What is the GCF of the number of pancakes and eggs?
A construction site has $12$ and $15$ meter long wooden planks. What is the LCM of these lengths?
A chef needs to package $48$ cookies into boxes that hold $8$ or $12$ cookies. What is the GCF of the number of cookies per box?
A builder is constructing a fence with $6$ and $9$ foot long sections. What is the LCM of these lengths?
A recipe for $2 \cdot 3$ cakes requires $5 \cdot 2$ cups of flour. What is the LCM of the number of cakes and cups of flour?
A construction crew needs to mix $20$ and $30$ liters of cement. What is the GCF of the amounts of cement?
A chef is preparing a dish that requires $9$ and $12$ cups of ingredients. What is the LCM of the number of cups?
A builder is designing a building with $8$ and $12$ foot high walls. What is the GCF of the wall heights?
A construction site has $24$ and $30$ meter long steel beams. Find the GCF and LCM of these lengths and explain how they can be used to design a stable structure.
A chef is preparing a recipe that requires $2 \cdot 3$ and $5 \cdot 2$ cups of ingredients. Find the LCM of the number of cups and explain how it can be used to scale up the recipe.

teacher_rules: Consider using visual aids to help students understand GCF and LCM concepts
Encourage students to use prime factorization to find GCF and LCM
Remind students to check their work by verifying that the GCF divides both numbers and the LCM is divisible by both numbers
